assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSMENT 1:
Three actionable concerns: (1) Safety-net hospital has substantially different patient characteristics from training data — unvalidated extension in 6 months creates distribution shift risk. (2) 80th percentile threshold selected on operational capacity, not cost-benefit analysis — the $45/$12,000 cost ratio creates a quantifiable break-even precision that should be verified before deployment. (3) HRRP penalty exposure depends on diagnosis-specific performance that has not been evaluated. All three are deployment-readiness issues. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSMENT 2:
The safety-net distribution shift is the most critical concern — deploying to a population with different payer mix, comorbidity burden, and social determinants without validation creates unquantified clinical risk. The threshold cost-benefit gap is analytically addressable pre-launch. HRRP diagnosis stratification is feasible using holdout data. None of these require model rejection but all require pre-deployment analysis. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSMENT 3:
All three identified concerns are genuine. The safety-net extension is the highest-risk element — it expands deployment to an unvalidated population within six months. The threshold should be validated against the cost-asymmetry structure before full deployment. HRRP-stratified analysis should be part of pre-deployment governance. The model is not ready for unrestricted deployment as described, but a structured phased deployment with pre-specified validation conditions is appropriate. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors: empirical_test_agreed. When synthesizing: if this case involves a contested empirical claim, an empirical test specification is required. Contested claim: whether the model performs adequately in the safety-net hospital population, and whether the current threshold is cost-justified. Empirical test specified below.

empirical_test.measure: Prospective model AUC-ROC and precision at 80th percentile threshold in safety-net hospital population; cost-benefit analysis comparing threshold precision to break-even ($45/$12,000 = 0.375% minimum precision for cost neutrality); HRRP diagnosis-stratified recall in holdout data
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC >= 0.70 (not substantially degraded vs. 0.76 in existing hospitals); threshold precision above 0.375% break-even; HRRP diagnosis groups show no systematic recall gap vs. overall model performance
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC < 0.65 or degraded by more than 10 percentage points vs. existing hospitals; threshold precision below break-even cost ratio; HRRP-relevant diagnoses systematically under-recalled compared to overall model
